data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp uses a microservices architecture to ensure scalability and independent deployment of services.
data.technical_spec: Each service runs in its own container within a Kubernetes cluster.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption Framework
data.description: The architecture supports end-to-end encryption for messages and calls to ensure data privacy and security.
data.technical_spec: Uses the Signal Protocol for encrypting messages.

data.title: Database Sharding
data.description: The application uses database sharding to efficiently manage and scale data storage.
data.technical_spec: Data is partitioned across multiple database instances using sharding keys.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Load balancers are used to distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Employs both hardware and software load balancers.

data.title: Distributed Architecture
data.description: Services are distributed across multiple data centers to ensure global availability and low latency.
data.technical_spec: Depends on a combination of edge servers and global data centers.
